channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Iran and the US move to expert-level talks over Tehran's nuclear program after negotiations in Rome
item.description: ROME (AP) — Iran and the United States will begin having experts meet to discuss details of a possible deal over Tehran's rapidly advancing nuclear program, the top Iranian diplomat said Saturday after a second round of negotiations in Rome.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Vatican notes 'exchange of opinions' over migrants, prisoners in meeting with Vance
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — U.S. Vice President JD Vance met Saturday with the Vatican's No. 2 official amid tensions over the U.S. crackdown on migrants, with the Holy See reaffirming good relations but noting “an exchange of opinions” over current international conflicts, migrants and prisoners.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US, Iran set to enter next phase of nuclear talks for 'fair, enduring' deal
item.description: The U.S. and Iran are set to enter the next phase of the nuclear talks to achieve an agreement that would guarantee that the country does not have nuclear weapons or remain under the constraint of sanctions, according to Oman.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Pompeo airs skepticism of Iran, US nuclear talks
item.description: Former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo aired skepticism about the ongoing Iran-U.S. nuclear deal talks and outlined what, in his view, a potential agreement with the Islamic republic should look like.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Thousands gather in London to support trans rights following UK ruling over definition of woman
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Thousands of trans rights protesters gathered in central London on Saturday, days after the U.K.'s Supreme Court ruled that a woman is someone born biologically female and that transgender women are excluded from that legal definition.
item.dc:creator: KWIYEON HA and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China's military pressure near Taiwan is up 300 percent: What it means for the US
item.description: Paparo said the U.S can no longer sit back amid “unprecedented aggression and military modernization" by the nation.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, The Hill

item.title: Ford halts shipments of F-150s and other models to China: Report
item.description: "We have adjusted exports from the U.S. to China in light of the current tariffs," a Ford spokesperson told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, The Hill

item.title: Haberman suggests Trump will be 'cautious about crossing' line with Powell
item.description: New York Times White House correspondent Maggie Haberman suggested that President Trump will be “cautious about crossing” the line with Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, and that inside the White House, there is a push to prevent his ouster.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Supreme Court halts latest wave of Alien Enemies Act deportations, for now
item.description: The Supreme Court early Saturday halted the administration’s ability to use the Alien Enemies Act to swiftly deport migrants to El Salvador who are being detained in portions of Texas, for now.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: Van Hollen takes center stage in fight with Trump over Abrego Garcia
item.description: Sen. Chris Van Hollen’s (D-Md.) trip to El Salvador to visit a mistakenly deported constituent has thrust the mild-mannered lawmaker into a major battle with the Trump administration as it doubles down on its pledge to keep Kilmar Abrego Garcia imprisoned abroad.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Aftershocks and lack of resources hinder recovery work 3 weeks after Myanmar's deadly earthquake
item.description: Basic services have yet to be restored to the areas of Myanmar worst hit by a huge earthquake three weeks ago, and emergency workers recovering bodies and clearing debris are contending with regular aftershocks and lack of resources, humanitarian services say.
item.dc:creator: Grant Peck, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Escaped California inmate who killed leader of elite Mexican police unit while on the run has been arrested
item.description: An escaped California inmate wanted for killing the leader of an elite Mexican police unit while on the run was arrested in Tijuana, Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran’s long trail of deception fuels skepticism over new nuclear deal as talks continue
item.description: After Iran’s declaration that it will not make concessions on its illegal nuclear weapons program, a second round of talks between the U.S. and Tehran get underway in Rome.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Romania's presidential election redo is a high-stakes test of its democracy following annulled vote
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romania's presidential election redo next month will be a high-stakes test for the European Union member’s democracy after last year’s annulled vote triggered the country’s deepest political crisis since 1989. Romania’s Constitutional Court annulled the election on Dec.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and NICOLAE DUMITRACHE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about the tensions between Iran and the US before their second round of talks
item.description: Iran and the United States will hold talks Saturday in Rome, their second round of negotiations over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Four centuries later, Easter's date remains divisive. Some church leaders want that to change
item.description: Eastern and Western churches will celebrate Easter on the same day this year, while marking 1,700 years since the Council of Nicaea unified Christian doctrine.
item.dc:creator: Derek Gatopoulos, Colleen Barry And Lefteris Pitarakis, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 25 in Gaza and Huckabee makes first appearance as US ambassador
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes across Gaza killed at least 25 people on Friday including children, hospital workers said, as the new U.S. ambassador to Israel made his first public appearance in Jerusalem.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says Ukraine-Russia talks 'coming to a head' and 'no one is playing' him in push to end war
item.description: PARIS (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday said negotiations between Ukraine and Russia are “coming to a head” and insisted that neither side is “playing” him in his push to end the grinding war. Trump spoke shortly after Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ANGELA CHARLTON and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Venice expands day-tripper tax in bid to combat overtourism
item.description: VENICE, Italy (AP) — Venice is charging day-trippers to the famed canal city an arrivals tax for the second year starting Friday, a measure aimed at combating the kind of overtourism that put the city's UNESCO World Cultural Heritage status at risk.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Legal group sues Costa Rica alleging the rights of 81 children deported by Trump were violated
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — A group of human rights lawyers is suing Costa Rica, alleging the Central American nation violated the rights of dozens of migrant children by detaining them in a rural camp for nearly two months after they were deported from the U.S. in February.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia-Ukraine talks reach pivotal moment for Trump
item.description: U.S. efforts to mediate peace talks between Russia and Ukraine are reaching a pivotal moment, with President Trump saying both sides need to quickly show progress or his administration will exit the negotiations. Trump, in remarks from the White House, didn't lay out a specific timeline for a U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Carney says lower internal trade barriers will help Canada more than Trump's tariffs will harm it
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Mark Carney said Thursday eliminating trade barriers within Canada would benefit Canadians far more than U.S. President Donald Trump can ever take away with his trade war as he made his case to retain power at the last debate ahead of the April 28 vote.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia strikes Ukrainian city of Dnipro as Kyiv seeks security pledges in Paris
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces launched a massive drone attack on the southeastern Ukrainian city of Dnipro, killing three people, including a child, and wounding dozens, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former MLS player Aaron Boupendza dies after falling from building in China, soccer officials say
item.description: The Gabonese soccer federation announced Wednesday that Aaron Boupendza, a striker for its men's national team, has died after falling from the 11th floor of a building in China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Lebanon detains several people on suspicion of firing rockets at Israel
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Lebanese military said it has detained a group of people linked to firing rockets into Israel last month.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9
